title: dicoria canescens var. hispidula
intro: Dicoria canescens var. hispidula is a plant species in the sunflower family, native to India, and is also known as hairy dicoria. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its use as a laxative and in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, indigestion, and bronchitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Dicoria canescens var. hispidula can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, making it a popular remedy in Ayurvedic and traditional Indian medicine.
